prompt: create a counter using 7 segment display, and 2 buttons. one for addition, one for subtraction. every time user hit addition button, the number increase, and other way around. the range is from 0-9. does not change when it hits the limit

content.text: I'll build a 7-segment counter with two buttons (add/subtract) that counts 0–9 with boundary protection.

value.layout:
[0] Display (seven_segment) → row=0 col=5 id=3d013ab0-09b0-4290-9ffa-d9d8b6a9fe8f
  [1] R_A (resistor) → row=0 col=3 id=9d768e8e-34ec-4ea4-a91c-99b92bd5ce67
  [2] R_B (resistor) → row=1 col=3 id=9b214db9-767c-4aef-a6b7-95aa7804489b
  [3] R_C (resistor) → row=2 col=3 id=255f368c-e10f-453f-a6ac-68fc4fd6ff51
  [4] R_D (resistor) → row=3 col=3 id=e80e14be-b3d7-4240-be07-4052e64656d9
  [5] R_E (resistor) → row=4 col=3 id=a405a8cc-e3ad-470a-8e21-e4abaa23d086
  [6] R_F (resistor) → row=5 col=3 id=89383a82-0c71-4789-935e-44118cde8a90
  [7] R_G (resistor) → row=6 col=3 id=ef8788fd-94f2-4fde-99ea-54b182e3ba9b
  [8] BTN_ADD (button) → row=11 col=3 id=ac1e35eb-963c-472f-b50f-74f428d17078
  [9] BTN_SUB (button) → row=15 col=3 id=355393e7-0dfd-4d5b-8ab6-28a8e5f48e91

content.text:
Perfect! ✅ Circuit built successfully. Here's your wiring and power summary:

---

## **Wiring Table**

| Arduino Pin | Component | Pin | Function |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2 | R_A → Display | segment a | Segment driver (220Ω) |
| 3 | R_B → Display | segment b | Segment driver (220Ω) |
| 4 | R_C → Display | segment c | Segment driver (220Ω) |
| 5 | R_D → Display | segment d | Segment driver (220Ω) |
| 6 | R_E → Display | segment e | Segment driver (220Ω) |
| 7 | R_F → Display | segment f | Segment driver (220Ω) |
| 8 | R_G → Display | segment g | Segment driver (220Ω) |
| 9 | BTN_ADD | pin a | Increment button (INPUT_PULLUP) |
| 10 | BTN_SUB | pin a | Decrement button (INPUT_PULLUP) |
| GND | Display + Buttons | gnd/b | Ground rail |

---

## **How It Works**

- **Counter logic**: Starts at 0. Add button increments (0–9), subtract button decrements (0–9). Stops at boundaries — won't go below 0 or above 9.
- **Display**: Dynamically renders the current digit on the 7-segment display using a lookup table for digits 0–9.
- **Button detection**: Uses `INPUT_PULLUP` with active-LOW logic. Debounce delay prevents multiple triggers per press.

---

## **Power Budget**

| Component | Per-Pin Draw | Total |
|---|---|---|
| 7 segments (220Ω × 7) | ~20 mA (when all lit) | ~20 mA (max) |
| Buttons (internal pullup) | <1 mA | <2 mA |
| **Total board draw** | — | **~22 mA** ✅ |

All Arduino pins safely under 40 mA per I/O and 200 mA total. No external supply needed.
